chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْإِشَارَةِ وَ النَّصِّ عَلَى الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 66 – The Indication and the wordings upon Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (293/ 3)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (293/ 3)

parts.text: زَيْدٌ الْيَمَامِيُّ قَالُوا حَدَّثَنَا شَهْرُ بْنُ حَوْشَبٍ

text: عَلِيّاً ( عليه السلام ) حِينَ سَارَ إِلَى الْكُوفَةِ اسْتَوْدَعَ أُمَّ سَلَمَةَ كُتُبَهُ وَ الْوَصِيَّةَ فَلَمَّا رَجَعَ الْحَسَنُ ( عليه السلام ) دَفَعَتْهَا إِلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Bakr Al Hazamy who said, ‘It was narrated to me by Al Ajlah, and Salma Bin Kuhayl, and Dawood Bin Abu Yazeed Al Yamami who (all) said:

‘Shahr Bin Howshab narrated to us that Ali<sup>asws</sup>, when he<sup>asws</sup> went to Al-Kufa, deposited with Umm Salma<sup>as</sup> his<sup>asws</sup> Books and the bequest. So when Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> returned (to Al-Medina), she<sup>as</sup> handed over these to him<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Sayf ibn ‘Amayra from abu Bakr al-Hadrami who has said that al-Ajlah, Salamah ibn Kuhayl, Dawud ibn abu Yazid and Zayd al-Yamami from Shahr ibn Hawshab who has said the following. “When Amir al-Mu’minin Ali (a.s.) was about to leave for Kufa he left in ’Umm Salama’s trust his books and his will. When al-Hassan (a.s.) returned to Madina she delivered all of them to him.” The above hadith is as follows in the book of al-Safwan:
